questions: You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your adult child calls to say they've decided to spend the holidays abroad with friends instead of coming home. How do you respond?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your closest friend declines to attend your milestone birthday party because they have a prior commitment. What do you say?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your partner tells you they want to spend the weekend at a retreat alone instead of going to a family event you'd invited them to. How do you handle it?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. A sibling says they can't help you care for your elderly parent this month because they're dealing with their own stressful situation. What do you tell them?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. A longtime friend says they won't be able to be in your wedding party because they can't afford the travel. How do you respond?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your colleague declines to stay late to help with a project even though you covered for them last quarter. What do you say?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your grown child tells you they've decided to move to another country for a job opportunity and won't be nearby anymore. How do you respond?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. A neighbor you've helped out many times says they can't return the favor right now when you ask for help moving furniture. What do you tell them?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your best friend says they're going on vacation the week of your surgery and won't be around to help you recover. How do you handle the conversation?

You believe in respecting people's choices and asking for what you need directly. Your sibling chooses to spend a significant inheritance on travel rather than contributing to a family expense you'd hoped they'd share. What do you say?
